response.justification: Sarah is a strong senior developer but a fundamental misfit for this role. She has only ~6 months of documented C++ experience (vs. the required 5–10 years), no Windows platform background, and zero capital markets or financial services domain expertise. Her career trajectory is web/full-stack development (Node.js, TypeScript, D Language), not capital markets systems. The gap requires retraining in multiple core competencies, making her unsuitable for a mid-senior capital markets role.
strengths: Demonstrated senior-level software engineering ability with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and international collaboration across diverse technical domains
Some SQL experience and exposure to Java, CI/CD pipelines, and microservices architecture
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ experience—only ~6 months documented (National Instruments) versus 5–10 years required; focus has been web/full-stack development
No Windows platform development background; career shows Linux/cloud-based systems; no SQL Server or Oracle expertise documented
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domain experience; RIDE Capital role focused on tech stack, not financial domain
